---
title: "02_Differential_abundance"
author: "Wanxin_Lai"
date: "2024-03-28"
output:
  rmarkdown::html_document:
    theme: lumen
    highlight: zenburn
    df_print: default
    toc: yes
    toc_float: TRUE
    keep_md: no
    code_folding: hide
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
setwd("C:/Users/awala/OneDrive - Norwegian University of Life Sciences/Documents/Small_Cow/MAG/")
load("./01_Filter_count.RData")
load("./02_differential_abundances.RData")
```

```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggtree)
library(phytools)
library(scales)
library(ggnewscale)
library(ggh4x)
library(gridExtra)
library(phyloseq)
library(ANCOMBC)
library(DESeq2)
library(DT)
library(cowplot)
library(ggpubr)
```

### PCA plot for T1 to T5
##### Filtered raw coverage with vst

```{r , message = FALSE}
metadata$Emission <- factor(metadata$Emission)
metadata <- metadata[colnames(count_table_cov), ]

dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(count_table_cov, colData = metadata, design = ~ Emission)

vsd <- varianceStabilizingTransformation(dds, blind=TRUE)
vsdd <- as.data.frame(assay(vsd))

raw.pca <- prcomp(t(vsdd))

summary(raw.pca)

q <- data.frame(raw.pca$x) %>% rownames_to_column %>% 
  left_join((metadata %>% rownames_to_column), by= join_by(rowname == rowname))

# calculate the variance explains:
# filt.pca$sdev^2 is our eigenvalue
var.expl <- raw.pca$sdev^2 / sum(raw.pca$sdev^2)*100

Time <- as.factor(q1$data$Time)
q1 <- ggplot(q, aes(PC1, PC2, col = Time, label = Animal)) + 
  xlab(paste0("PC1 (", round(var.expl[1], digits=2),")")) +
  ylab(paste0("PC2 (", round(var.expl[2], digits=2),")")) +
  geom_point()  + 
  geom_text(hjust=0.6, vjust=1.3, size = 3) +
  theme_classic()

Emission <- as.factor(q2$data$Emission)
Vsplit <- as.factor(q$vsplit_old)
Breed <- as.factor(q2$data$Breed)
q2 <- ggplot(q, aes(PC1, PC2, col = Emission, shape = Breed)) + 
  xlab(paste0("PC1 (", round(var.expl[1], digits=2),")")) +
  ylab(paste0("PC2 (", round(var.expl[2], digits=2),")")) +
  geom_point() +
  theme(axis.title.y=element_blank()) +
  theme_classic()


q3 <- ggplot(q, aes(PC1, PC2, col = Breed)) + 
  xlab(paste0("PC1 (", round(var.expl[1], digits=2),")")) +
  ylab(paste0("PC2 (", round(var.expl[2], digits=2),")")) +
  geom_point()


#q4 <- ggplot(q, aes(PC1, PC2, col = Vsplit)) + 
  xlab(paste0("PC1 (", round(var.expl[1], digits=2),")")) +
  ylab(paste0("PC2 (", round(var.expl[2], digits=2),")")) +
  geom_point()

#q5 <- ggplot(q, aes(PC3, PC4, col = as.factor(vsplit_old))) + 
  xlab(paste0("PC3 (", round(var.expl[3], digits=2),")")) +
  ylab(paste0("PC4 (", round(var.expl[4], digits=2),")")) +
  geom_point()

plot_grid(q1, q2, align = "hv")#, q3, q4), q5)

```

Split reappeared.

## Finding driving MAGs of PC1 and PC2

```{r}
# Inspect genes that are responsible for PC1 to PC1 with the highest loading
# Range of rotation from abs value 1 ans -1 indicates positive and negative correlation between the dims and pc 
# we don't do sort(pc$rotation[,1], decreasing=T) because this ignore those with -ve correlations.

# set a cut off to filter important for loadings
loading_cutoff <- sqrt(1/ncol(t(vsdd)))

# Create an empty list for those that pass the cutoff
passing_rownames <- vector()

# setting keep_row = FALSE
for(pc_row in 1:nrow(raw.pca$rotation)) {
  keep_row <- FALSE
  
  # for the first 1 PCs
  for (pc_col in 1:2) {
    if (abs(raw.pca$rotation[pc_row, pc_col]) > loading_cutoff) {
      keep_row <- TRUE
    }
  }
  
  if (keep_row == TRUE) {
    passing_rownames <- c(passing_rownames, rownames(raw.pca$rotation)[pc_row])
  }
}

# now we know which genes have the most influence on each selected PC axis.
cat(paste(length(passing_rownames), " features/MAGs pass the loading cutoff of ", round(loading_cutoff, 4), sep = "" ))

```
Below shows the most influential selected MAGs that contribute to the saparation between T5 and other Ts:
```{r table of driving mags}
top_loadings <- raw.pca$rotation[passing_rownames, ][,1]

# new data with reduced dimensions
raw.new.data <- data.frame(raw.pca$x[,1] %*% t(top_loadings))

#can change to 300 if you like.
raw.200 <- t(raw.new.data) %>% 
  data.frame() %>%
  rownames_to_column(var = "Genome") %>%
  left_join(., mags_details, by = join_by(Genome == Genome)) %>% 
  select(Genome, Domain, Phylum, Genus, Species, Contamination, Completeness, Genome_Size)

datatable(raw.200)
```

#### Master table prior to generate Relative Abundance value based on taxa level.
```{r, fig.width= 15}

preRA <- count_table_cov_size %>% 
  rownames_to_column(var="Genome") %>% 
  mutate_at(vars(-Genome),~./sum(.)) %>% #apply TSS
  pivot_longer(-Genome, names_to = "Sample", values_to = "Count") %>% #reduce to minimum number of columns
  merge(., metadata, by.x = "Sample", by.y = 0, all.x = T) %>% #append sample metadata
  left_join(., (mags_details %>% select(Genome, Domain, Phylum, Genus)), by = join_by(Genome == Genome)) %>% # Add taxo info#
  left_join(., (mags_phylum_genomes_sorted %>% select(Genome, classification, color)),by = join_by(Genome == Genome)) # Add colour info
```




##### Plot of low vs high 313 MAGs
```{r 313 MAGs plot, message=FALSE}
summary_200 <- preRA %>% 
  filter(Genome %in% raw.200$Genome) %>% 
  group_by(Sample, Phylum) %>% 
  summarise(relabun=sum(Count))# new RA of MAG based on phylum and emission group

# Have a look at the mean relative abundance per phylum and standard deviation
summary_200 %>%
  group_by(Phylum) %>% 
  summarise(mean=mean(relabun), sd = sd(relabun)) %>%
  arrange(-mean)

#determine arrangement
phylum_200arrange <- summary_200 %>%
    group_by(Phylum) %>%
    summarise(mean=mean(relabun)) %>%
    arrange(-mean) %>%
    select(Phylum) %>%
    pull()

summary_200 %>% 
  mutate(log_relabun = log10(relabun+1)) %>% 
  left_join(preRA %>% select(Sample, Emission, Phylum, Genus, color), by=c("Sample", "Phylum")) %>%
  mutate(Phylum = factor(Phylum, levels=phylum_200arrange)) %>%
  unite("Emission", c(Phylum, Emission),remove = FALSE) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(y = log_relabun, x = Phylum, group=Emission, fill=Phylum)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = phylum_colour) +
      geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.6) +
      #geom_point(alpha=0.5) +
      #facet_wrap(. ~ , scales = "free", ncol=4) +
      coord_cartesian(xlim = c(1, NA)) +
      theme_classic() +
      stat_compare_means(aes(label = ..p.signif..),
                     #label.y = c(seq( 0.6, by = 0, length.out = 4)),
                     tip.length = 0, vjust = 0.5) + #Compare low vs high in every phyl
      theme(
        strip.background = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor.x = element_line(size = .1, color = "grey"),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.title.y = element_text(),
        axis.text.x = element_blank()) +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(override.aes= list(alpha = 0.4)))
  ylab("log10(RA)+1")
```


##### Checking the time series compositions of the driving MAGs at phylum tax.
```{r, fig.height=5, fig.width=11}
summary_200_time <- preRA %>% 
  filter(Genome %in% raw.200$Genome) %>% 
  group_by(Sample, Phylum) %>% 
  summarise(relabun=sum(Count))# new RA of MAG based on phylum and emission group

# Have a look at the mean relative abundance per phylum and standard deviation
summary_200_time %>%
  group_by(Phylum) %>% 
  summarise(mean=mean(relabun), sd = sd(relabun)) %>%
  arrange(-mean)

#determine arrangement
phylum_200arrange <- summary_200_time %>%
    group_by(Phylum) %>%
    summarise(mean=mean(relabun)) %>%
    arrange(-mean) %>%
    select(Phylum) %>%
    pull()

summary_200_time %>% 
  mutate(log_relabun = log10(relabun+1)) %>% 
  left_join(preRA %>% select(Sample, Emission, Phylum, Genus, color, Time), by=c("Sample", "Phylum")) %>%
  mutate(Phylum = factor(Phylum, levels=phylum_200arrange)) %>%
  unite("Emission", c(Phylum, Emission),remove = FALSE) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(y = log_relabun, x = Phylum, group=Emission, fill=Phylum)) +
      geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.5) +
      #geom_point(alpha=0.5) +
      facet_wrap(. ~ Time, scales = "free", ncol=5) +
      coord_cartesian(xlim = c(1, NA)) +
      theme_classic() +
      scale_fill_manual(values = phylum_colour)+
      stat_compare_means(aes(label = ..p.signif..),
                     #label.y = c(seq( 0.6, by = 0, length.out = 4)),
                     tip.length = 0, vjust = 0.5) + #Compare low vs high in every phyl
      theme(
        legend.position = "bottom",
        strip.background = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor.x = element_line(size = .1, color = "grey"),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.title.y = element_text(),
        axis.text.x = element_blank()) +
  ylab("log10(RA)+1")
  
```

Bacteriodota has a strong non monotnic pattern...At T5, methanogen is very higher for high emission group, while bacteriodota is lower compared to low emission.

##### Enough split of 200MAGs from  T1T5, now look at the low vs high methane emission of all 700 MAGs

## Create phyloseq object
### AncomBC2
Fitting linear model into the formula. High emission as the beta coef.
```{r filt count phyloseq, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Create phyloseq objet

# Convert to phyloseq sample_data object
phylo_samples <- metadata %>%
  sample_data() 

phylo_genomes <- count_table_cov %>%
  mutate_all(~ replace(., . == 0, 0.00001)) %>% # add pseudo counts to avoid structural zero, need improvement!
  mutate_all(~./sum(.)) %>% #apply TSS nornalisation
  otu_table(., taxa_are_rows = TRUE)

phylo_taxonomy <- mags_details %>%
  filter(Genome %in% rownames(phylo_genomes)) %>% # remove structural zeros
  mutate(Genome2 = Genome) %>% # create a pseudo genome column 
  column_to_rownames("Genome2") %>%
  dplyr::select(Domain, Phylum, Genus, Species, Genome) %>% # add an additional taxonomic level to ensure genome-level analysis (as no all genomes have species-level taxonomic assignments. Otherwise, ANCOMBC2 aggregates analyses per species)
  as.matrix() %>%
  tax_table() # convert to phyloseq tax_table object

phyloseq_dt <- phyloseq(phylo_genomes, phylo_taxonomy, phylo_samples)

# For merging with ouput later
tax <- data.frame(phyloseq_dt@tax_table) %>%
  rownames_to_column(., "taxon")
```


#### Taxonomic abundance (genus level) of 700 MAGs between low and high emission group (T1-T5)
##### Glance at the top 20 genus with highest mean value
```{r top mean of 700 mags, message = FALSE, fig.height=6}

summary_genus <- preRA %>% 
  group_by(Sample, Genus) %>% 
  summarise(relabun=sum(Count))# new RA of MAG based on phylum and emission group

# Have a look at the mean relative abundance per genus and standard deviation
top20genus <- summary_genus %>%
  group_by(Genus) %>% 
  summarise(mean=mean(relabun), sd = sd(relabun)) %>%
  arrange(-mean) %>% 
  top_n(20, wt = mean)

datatable(summary_genus %>%
  group_by(Genus) %>% 
  summarise(mean=mean(relabun), sd = sd(relabun)) %>%
  arrange(-mean) )

#determine arrangement
genus_arrange <- summary_genus %>%
   filter(Genus %in% unique(top20genus$Genus)) %>% 
    group_by(Genus) %>%
    summarise(mean=mean(relabun)) %>%
    arrange(-mean) %>%
    select(Genus) %>%
    pull()

summary_genus %>% 
  filter(Genus %in% unique(top20genus$Genus)) %>% 
  mutate(log_relabun = log2(relabun+1)) %>% 
  left_join(preRA %>% select(Sample, Emission, Phylum, Genus, color, Time), by=join_by("Sample", "Genus")) %>%
  mutate(Genus = factor(Genus, levels=genus_arrange)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Genus)) %>% 
  
  ggplot(aes(x=log_relabun, y=Genus, group=Genus, color = color)) +
        scale_color_identity() +
        geom_point(alpha=0.5, aes(x = log_relabun, y = Genus, color = color)) +
        geom_boxplot(aes(x=log_relabun, y = Genus)) +
        facet_wrap(.~Emission)+
theme_minimal() +
  coord_flip() +
    theme(legend.position = "right", axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 50, vjust = 0.5, hjust=1, margin = margin(t=-25), size = 7), axis.text.y = element_text(size=7), panel.spacing = unit(2.5, "lines")) +
  xlab("log2(RA+1)")

```

Genus Prevotella looks distinctly different between the emissions

##### In the time series of top 20 genus with highest mean
```{r fig.height=9}

top_20_pl <- summary_genus %>% 
  filter(Genus %in% unique(top20genus$Genus[1:20])) %>% 
  mutate(log_relabun = log2(relabun+1)) %>% 
  left_join(preRA %>% select(Sample, Emission, Phylum, Genus, color, Time), by=join_by("Sample", "Genus")) %>%
  mutate(Genus = factor(Genus, levels=genus_arrange)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Genus)) %>% 
  
  ggplot(aes(x=log_relabun, y=Genus, group=Genus, color = color)) +
        scale_color_identity() +
        geom_point(alpha=0.5, aes(x = log_relabun, y = Genus, color = color)) +
        geom_boxplot(aes(x=log_relabun, y = Genus)) +
        facet_wrap(.~Emission + Time, ncol = 5, nrow = 2) +
theme_minimal() +
  coord_flip() +
    theme(legend.position = "right", axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust=1, size = 7), axis.text.y = element_text(size=7)) +
  xlab("log2(RA+1)")

top_20_pl
```

```{r}
top_20_genus <- summary_genus %>% 
  filter(Genus %in% unique(top20genus$Genus[1:20])) %>% 
  mutate(log_relabun = log2(relabun+1)) %>% 
  left_join(preRA %>% select(Sample, Emission, Phylum, Genus, Genome, color, Time), by=join_by("Sample", "Genus")) %>%
  mutate(Genus = factor(Genus, levels=genus_arrange)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Genus))

save(top_20_genus, top_20_pl, file = "C:/Users/awala/OneDrive - Norwegian University of Life Sciences/Documents/Small_Cow/MAG/top-20-genus-Timeseries.RData")
```


## Differential abundance (genus level)

Continuous covariates: â€śtimeâ€ť, "breed"

Categorical covariates: â€śbreedâ€ť

The group variable of interest: â€śEmissionâ€ť
```{r warning = FALSE, eval = FALSE}
emis_da.genus <- ancombc2(data = phyloseq_dt, assay_name = "counts", tax_level = "Genus", 
         fix_formula = "Breed + Time + Emission", p_adj_method = "fdr",
        rand_formula = "(1|Animal)",
         pseudo_sens = FALSE, # sensitivity analysis to the pseudo-count addition, n <50
         # watch out for this line ! prv_cut = 0.10, lib_cut = 1000, s0_perc = 0.05,
         prv_cut = 0,# prevelance cut
         lib_cut = 0,#library_size
         s0_perc = 0.01,
         group = "Emission",
         struc_zero = FALSE,# Whether to detect structural zeros
         neg_lb = FALSE,# Whether to classify a taxon as a structural zero
         alpha = 0.05,
         n_cl = 2, # The number of nodes to be forked.
         verbose = TRUE, # generate verbose output during the ANCOM-BC2 fitting process?
         global = FALSE,#  test whether there exists at least one group effect that is significantly different from others
         pairwise = FALSE,
         dunnet = FALSE,
         trend = FALSE,
         iter_control = list(tol = 1e-5, max_iter = 20, verbose = FALSE),
         #em_control = list(tol = 1e-5, max_iter = 100), # E-M algorithm
         lme_control = lme4::lmerControl(),#mixed model fitting
         #mdfdr_control = list(fwer_ctrl_method = "fdr", B = 5),#mixed directional false discover rate, for performning multiple pairwise comparisons, not very important to apply here
         trend_control = NULL
)
```

#### 3 Genus which are differentially abundant between low vs high:

```{r}
emis_da_genus_table <- emis_da.genus$res %>%
   dplyr::select(Genus=taxon, lfc_Emissionlow, p_Emissionlow, q_Emissionlow) %>%
   filter(p_Emissionlow < 0.05) %>%
   merge(., tax, by.x = "Genus", by.y = "taxon", all.x = T)


emis_da.genus$res %>%
  select(Genus=taxon, lfc_Emissionlow, p_Emissionlow) %>%
  filter(p_Emissionlow < 0.05) %>%
  arrange(lfc_Emissionlow) %>%
  left_join(tax %>% select(-c(taxon,Genome,Species)) %>% unique(), by = join_by(Genus==Genus))
  
```

```{r}
summary_genus %>% 
  filter(Genus %in% emis_da_genus_table$Genus) %>% 
  mutate(log_relabun = log2(relabun+1)) %>% 
  left_join(preRA %>% select(Sample, Emission, Phylum, Genus, color, Time), by=join_by("Sample", "Genus")) %>%
  mutate(Genus = factor(Genus)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Genus)) %>% 
  
  ggplot(aes(x=log_relabun, y=Genus, group=Genus, color = color)) +
        scale_color_identity() +
        geom_point(alpha=0.5, aes(x = log_relabun, y = Genus, color = color)) +
        geom_boxplot(aes(x=log_relabun, y = Genus)) +
        facet_wrap(.~Emission, ncol = 5, nrow = 2) +
theme_minimal() +
  coord_flip() +
    theme(legend.position = "right", axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust=1, size = 7), axis.text.y = element_text(size=7)) +
  xlab("log2(RA+1)")
```

Hmm...??!

## Differential Abundance analysis at MAG level

```{r filt count ancombc, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE, eval = FALSE}
emis_da <- ancombc2(data = phyloseq_dt, assay_name = "counts", tax_level = NULL, 
         fix_formula = "Breed + Time + Emission", p_adj_method = "fdr",
        rand_formula = "(1|Animal)",
         pseudo_sens = FALSE, # sensitivity analysis to the pseudo-count addition, n <50
         # watch out for this line ! prv_cut = 0.10, lib_cut = 1000, s0_perc = 0.05,
         prv_cut = 0,# prevelance cut
         lib_cut = 0,#library_size
         s0_perc = 0.05,
         group = "Emission",
         struc_zero = FALSE,# Whether to detect structural zeros
         neg_lb = FALSE,# Whether to classify a taxon as a structural zero
         alpha = 0.05,
         n_cl = 2, # The number of nodes to be forked.
         verbose = TRUE, # generate verbose output during the ANCOM-BC2 fitting process?
         global = FALSE,#  test whether there exists at least one group effect that is significantly different from others
         pairwise = FALSE,
         dunnet = FALSE,
         trend = FALSE,
         iter_control = list(tol = 1e-5, max_iter = 20, verbose = FALSE),
         #em_control = list(tol = 1e-5, max_iter = 100), # E-M algorithm
         lme_control = lme4::lmerControl(),#mixed model fitting
         #mdfdr_control = list(fwer_ctrl_method = "fdr", B = 5),#mixed directional false discover rate, for performning multiple pairwise comparisons, not very important to apply here
         trend_control = NULL
)
```


```{r raw concatenation}

emis_da_table <- emis_da$res %>%
  dplyr::select(genome=taxon, lfc_Emissionlow, p_Emissionlow, q_Emissionlow) %>%
  filter(p_Emissionlow < 0.05) %>%
  merge(., tax, by.x = "genome", by.y = "taxon", all.x = T)

datatable(emis_da_table %>%
  select(Genome, Phylum, Genus,lfc_Emissionlow, p_Emissionlow, q_Emissionlow))
```

6 MAGs were discovered with ANCOMBC2 dierenially abundant between low and high emission.

```{r}
 emis_da$res %>%
  dplyr::select(genome=taxon, lfc_Emissionlow, p_Emissionlow, q_Emissionlow) %>%
  filter(p_Emissionlow < 0.05) %>%
  merge(., tax, by.x = "genome", by.y = "taxon", all.x = T)
```


### DESeq2
#### Test with genome count
```{r deseq2, message = FALSE}

res <- DESeq(dds)

res_all_count <- results(res) 

res_all_count <- res_all_count[order(res_all_count$padj, na.last=NA), ]

alpha = 0.05

sigtax_all_count_thres <- res_all_count[(res_all_count$pvalue < alpha), ]

sigtax_all_count_thres <- cbind(as(sigtax_all_count_thres, "data.frame"), as(tax_table(phyloseq_dt)[rownames(sigtax_all_count_thres), ], "matrix"))

cat(nrow(sigtax_all_count_thres)," MAGs were discovered using DESeq2.\n")
datatable(sigtax_all_count_thres[,c(8,9,1,2,6)])
```




#### Formating count table for plots

```{r format filt count tables}
# 2 choices:
# (i) Count table with log2 +1 transformation
merged_count <- count_table_cov %>%
  rownames_to_column("Genome") %>%

  #reduce to minimum number of columns
  pivot_longer(-Genome, names_to = "Sample", values_to = "Count") %>% 
  merge(., metadata, by.x = "Sample", by.y = 0, all.x = T) %>% #append sample metadata
  left_join(., preRA %>% select(Genome, Phylum) %>% unique(), by = join_by(Genome == Genome)) %>% # Phylum and color
  mutate(log_count = log2(Count+1))


normalised_merged_count <- vsdd %>%
  rownames_to_column("Genome") %>%

  #reduce to minimum number of columns
  pivot_longer(-Genome, names_to = "Sample", values_to = "Count") %>% 
  merge(., metadata, by.x = "Sample", by.y = 0, all.x = T) %>% #append sample metadata
  left_join(., preRA %>% select(Genome, Phylum) %>% unique(), by = join_by(Genome == Genome)) %>% # Phylum and color
  mutate(log_count = log2(Count+1))
```


```{r Bias correct table}
# (ii)
# Abundance table after AncomBC2 bias correction
bias_correct_log_table = emis_da$bias_correct_log_table
# By default, ANCOM-BC2 does not add pseudo-counts to zero counts, which can 
# result in NAs in the bias-corrected log abundances. Users have the option to 
# either leave the NAs as they are or replace them with zeros. 
# This replacement is equivalent to adding pseudo-counts of ones to the zero counts. 
bias_correct_log_table[is.na(bias_correct_log_table)] = 0

#reduce to minimum number of columns
bias_correct_log_table_pivot <- bias_correct_log_table %>% 
  rownames_to_column("Genome") %>% 
  pivot_longer(-Genome, names_to = "Sample", values_to = "log_abundances") %>% 
  merge(., metadata, by.x = "Sample", by.y = 0, all.x = T) %>% #append sample metadata
  left_join(., preRA %>% select(Genome, Phylum) %>% unique(), by = join_by(Genome == Genome))


# Show the first 6 samples
round(bias_correct_log_table[, 1:6], 2) %>% 
  datatable(caption = "Bias-corrected log abundances")

```


#### MAGs found in both DESeq2 and AncomBC2
```{r}
shared <- sigtax_all_count_thres[!is.na(match(rownames(sigtax_all_count_thres), emis_da_table$Genome)),]
cat(nrow(shared), "is/are found in both methods:\n")

datatable(sigtax_all_count_thres[!is.na(match(rownames(sigtax_all_count_thres), emis_da_table$genome)),])
```
The FC value comes from DESeq2

#### The Mags found in both ANCOMBC2 and DESeq2
```{r shared ancom deseq, fig.height= 3, fig.width=8}
#summary(res_all_count)
pl_list <-list()

#layout(matrix(c(1,1,2,3), 6, 2, byrow = TRUE))
for(i in 1:nrow(shared)){
  
  nameMAGs <- rownames(shared)[i]
  Phylum <- merged_count[merged_count$Genome == nameMAGs,]$Phylum
  

  p <- ggplot(merged_count %>% filter(Genome %in% nameMAGs), aes(x=Emission, y = log_count, group = Emission, color = Emission)) +
    geom_boxplot() +
    geom_point() +
    #scale_y_continuous(label=scientific_format()) +
    xlab(paste(nameMAGs,Phylum, sep = ": "))
  p <- p + ylab("Log2(Count+1)")  
  p <- p + theme(axis.title.x = element_text(size = 9), axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9), axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12))
  #print(p)
  
  pl_list[[i]] <- p
  
}

plot_grid(plotlist = pl_list, ncol = 3)
```

##### Uniq to DESeq2
##### VSDD 
```{r fig.height= 35, fig.width=12}
uniq.ds <- sigtax_all_count_thres[is.na(match(rownames(sigtax_all_count_thres), rownames(shared))),]

pl_list <-list()

#layout(matrix(c(1,1,2,3), 6, 2, byrow = TRUE))
for(i in 1:nrow(uniq.ds)){
  
  nameMAGs <- rownames(uniq.ds)[i]
  Phylum <- normalised_merged_count[normalised_merged_count$Genome == nameMAGs,]$Phylum

  p <- ggplot(normalised_merged_count %>% filter(Genome %in% nameMAGs), aes(x=Emission, y = log_count, group = Emission, color = Emission)) +
    geom_boxplot() +
    geom_point() +
    #scale_y_continuous(label=scientific_format()) +
    xlab(paste(nameMAGs,Phylum, sep = ": "))
  p <- p + ylab("Normalised Count)")  
  p <- p + theme(axis.title.x = element_text(size = 9), axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9), axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12))
  #print(p)
  
  pl_list[[i]] <- p
  
}

plot_grid(plotlist = pl_list, ncol = 3)
```

Many unrealiable resuls....

##### swap with log2 transformed count table
```{r  fig.height= 35, fig.width=12, eval=FALSE}
uniq.ds <- sigtax_all_count_thres[is.na(match(rownames(sigtax_all_count_thres), rownames(shared))),]

pl_list <-list()

#layout(matrix(c(1,1,2,3), 6, 2, byrow = TRUE))
for(i in 1:nrow(uniq.ds)){
  
  nameMAGs <- rownames(uniq.ds)[i]
  Phylum <- merged_count[merged_count$Genome == nameMAGs,]$Phylum

  p <- ggplot(merged_count %>% filter(Genome %in% nameMAGs), aes(x=Emission, y = log_count, group = Emission, color = Emission)) +
    geom_boxplot() +
    geom_point() +
    #scale_y_continuous(label=scientific_format()) +
    xlab(paste(nameMAGs,Phylum, sep = ": "))
  p <- p + ylab("Log2(Count+1)")  
  p <- p + theme(axis.title.x = element_text(size = 9), axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9), axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12))
  #print(p)
  
  pl_list[[i]] <- p
  
}

plot_grid(plotlist = pl_list, ncol = 3)
```

I will not show them here, equally bad.

##### Uniq to AncomBC2
```{r uniqancom, fig.height= 10, fig.width=18}

uniq.ac <- data.frame(emis_da_table[is.na(match(emis_da_table$genome, rownames(shared))),], row.names = 1)

#summary(res_all_count)
pl_list <-list()

#layout(matrix(c(1,1,2,3), 6, 2, byrow = TRUE))
for(i in 1:nrow(uniq.ac)){
  
  nameMAGs <- rownames(uniq.ac)[i]
  Phylum <- bias_correct_log_table_pivot[bias_correct_log_table_pivot$Genome == nameMAGs,]$Phylum
  
  p <- ggplot(bias_correct_log_table_pivot %>% filter(Genome %in% nameMAGs), aes(x=Emission, y = log_abundances, group = Emission, color = Emission)) +
    geom_boxplot() +
    geom_point() +
    scale_y_continuous(label=scientific_format()) +
    xlab(paste(nameMAGs,Phylum, sep = ": "))
  p <- p +  ylab("Log2(Count+1)") 
  p <- p + theme(axis.title.x = element_text(size = 10), axis.title.y = element_blank(), axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10), axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12))
  
  pl_list[[i]] <- p
  
}

plot_grid(plotlist = pl_list, ncol = 3)
```

```{r}
mags_phylum_genomes_sorted %>% select(Phylum, color) %>% unique()
```

```{r warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
library("ggplot2")
theme_set(theme_bw())

sigtabgen <- subset(sigtax_all_count_thres, !is.na(Genus)) %>% left_join(preRA %>% select(Phylum, color), by = join_by(Phylum == Phylum))

# Phylum order
x = tapply(sigtabgen$log2FoldChange, sigtabgen$Phylum, function(x) max(x))
x = sort(x, TRUE)
sigtabgen$Phylum = factor(as.character(sigtabgen$Phylum), levels=names(x))

# Genus order
x = tapply(sigtabgen$log2FoldChange, sigtabgen$Genus, function(x) max(x))
x = sort(x, TRUE)
sigtabgen$Genus = factor(as.character(sigtabgen$Genus), levels=names(x))

Phylum_colour <- data.frame(unique(preRA[, c("Phylum", "color")]), row.names = NULL)
phylum_colour <- split(Phylum_colour$color, Phylum_colour$Phylum)
#setNames(Phylum_colour$color, Phylum_colour$Phylum)


dsraw <-  ggplot(sigtabgen, aes(x=Genus, y=log2FoldChange, color = Phylum)) + 
  #geom_vline(yintercept = 0.0, color = "gray") +
  geom_point(size=5) + 
  scale_color_manual(values = phylum_colour) +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(angle = -90, hjust = 0, vjust=0.5), axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 50,  vjust = 0.5, hjust=1, margin = margin(t=-30))) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "darkgrey")+
  xlab("Genus") +
  ylab("log2FoldChange")
```

```{r}
plotMA(res, main = "DESeq2 volcano plot")
```

#### Obtain bias-corrected abundances from AncomBC2
It is important to acknowledge that the estimation of sampling fractions in ANCOM-BC2 is limited to an additive constant. This means that only the difference between bias-corrected log abundances is meaningful, rather than the absolute values themselves.

Moreover, within each taxon, the bias-corrected log abundances are centered across samples. ANCOM-BC2 operates on the assumption that while these taxon-specific biases differ between taxa, they stay consistent within a given taxon across various samples. This assumption enables ANCOM-BC2 to accommodate intra-taxon variations through the centering of the bias-corrected log abundances.

```{r}

lg_corr_pivot <- data.frame(bias_correct_log_table) %>% 
 rownames_to_column("Genome") %>%
 #reduce to minimum number of columns
  pivot_longer(-Genome, names_to = "Sample", values_to = "log_abundances") %>%
  group_by(Genome) %>% 
  summarise(mean=mean(log_abundances)) %>% 
  left_join(emis_da$res %>% select(taxon, lfc_Emissionlow, p_Emissionlow), by=join_by(Genome == taxon))

lg_corr_pivot$significance <- ifelse(lg_corr_pivot$p_Emissionlow < 0.05, "Significant", "Not Significant")

# Create the volcano plot
volcano_plot <- ggplot(lg_corr_pivot, aes(x = mean, y = lfc_Emissionlow, color = significance)) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Significant" = "blue", "Not Significant" = "grey")) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") + # Add significance threshold (adjust as needed)
  labs(x = "mean of normalised abundance", y = "Log10FC", title = "AncomBC2") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))

# Display the plot
print(volcano_plot)
```

I am not sure if this is wrong.

```{r ancom lgFC plot}
acraw<- ggplot(emis_da_table, aes(x = Genus,
                          y = lfc_Emissionlow, color = Phylum)) +
  geom_point(size = 6) +
  #scale_color_manual(values = phylum_colors[-c(3,4)]) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0) +
  scale_color_manual(values = phylum_colour) +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(angle = -90, hjust = 0, vjust=0.5), axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 50,  vjust = 0.5, hjust=1, margin = margin(t=-20))) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "darkgrey")+
  xlab("Genus") +
  ylab("log10FoldChange") +
  guides(col = guide_legend("Phylum"))
```


```{r , warning = FALSE, fig.width= 10, fig.height=8}

raw.da <- plot_grid(acraw, dsraw, ncols = 2, rel_widths = c(2, 2))

title <- ggdraw() + draw_label( "AncomBC vs DESeq2 DA (MAGs)", fontface='bold')

plot_grid(title, raw.da, ncol=1, rel_heights=c(0.1, 1)) # rel_heights values control title margins
```

#### Plot showing the phyla of MAGs that are considered differentially abundant from ANCOMBC
```{r}
emis_da$res %>%
  na.omit() %>%
  dplyr::select(Genome=taxon, lfc_Emissionlow, p_Emissionlow) %>%
  left_join(preRA, by = join_by(Genome == Genome)) %>%
  mutate(Phylum = ifelse(p_Emissionlow < 0.05, Phylum, NA)) %>%
  ggplot(., aes(x = lfc_Emissionlow, y = -log(p_Emissionlow), color = Phylum)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log(0.05), colour = "grey") +
  #xlim(c(-10,4)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = phylum_colour) +
  labs(color = "Significannt phyla", x = "Log-fold difference between emission group", y = "p-value") +
  theme_classic()

```


## Differential abundance at the phylum level
```{r warning = FALSE}
emis_da.phylum <- ancombc2(data = phyloseq_dt, assay_name = "counts", tax_level = "Phylum", 
         fix_formula = "Breed + Time + Emission", p_adj_method = "fdr",
        rand_formula = "(1|Animal)",
         pseudo_sens = FALSE, # sensitivity analysis to the pseudo-count addition, n <50
         # watch out for this line ! prv_cut = 0.10, lib_cut = 1000, s0_perc = 0.05,
         prv_cut = 0,# prevelance cut
         lib_cut = 0,#library_size
         s0_perc = 0.05,
         group = "Emission",
         struc_zero = FALSE,# Whether to detect structural zeros
         neg_lb = FALSE,# Whether to classify a taxon as a structural zero
         alpha = 0.05,
         n_cl = 2, # The number of nodes to be forked.
         verbose = TRUE, # generate verbose output during the ANCOM-BC2 fitting process?
         global = FALSE,#  test whether there exists at least one group effect that is significantly different from others
         pairwise = FALSE,
         dunnet = FALSE,
         trend = FALSE,
         iter_control = list(tol = 1e-5, max_iter = 20, verbose = FALSE),
         #em_control = list(tol = 1e-5, max_iter = 100), # E-M algorithm
         lme_control = lme4::lmerControl(),#mixed model fitting
         #mdfdr_control = list(fwer_ctrl_method = "fdr", B = 5),#mixed directional false discover rate, for performning multiple pairwise comparisons, not very important to apply here
         trend_control = NULL)

```

```{r}
emis_da_phylum_table <- emis_da.phylum$res %>%
   dplyr::select(Phylum=taxon, lfc_Emissionlow, p_Emissionlow, q_Emissionlow) %>%
   filter(p_Emissionlow < 0.01) 

emis_da_phylum_table
```
No sure if AMCOMBC2 is too stringent or there are fine tuning required. 
Maybe should try using t-test for every Genus/Phylum between high vs low emission and limited to T4 and T5.... DESeq2 seems woking well but not sure how to proceed.
Will explore the alpha and beta diversities of samples later.
```{r}
save(preRA, 
     count_table_cov,
     metadata, 
     count_table_cov_size,
     mags_details,
     phylum_colour,
     emis_da.genus,
     emis_da,
     bias_correct_log_table,
     bias_correct_log_table_pivot,
     raw.200,
     file = "./02_differential_abundances.RData")
```



```
